skill.name: pencil-to-code
skill.description: Convert Pencil .pen design files and named Pencil node IDs into production frontend code with strong visual fidelity.
skill.summary: Turn Pencil design handoffs into responsive frontend implementations.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, shell execution, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown code spans, CSS terms, and design workflow prose. No prompt injection or malicious behavior was found. The only confirmed concern is a hardcoded third-party promotional URL, which is low risk because it does not trigger automatic network access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain design-to-code prose, UI terms, or CSS guidance. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, or security-sensitive crypto usage appears in these files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text is documentation and YAML display metadata. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any crypto API usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Detections
low_findings.description: The backtick matches are Markdown inline code or a browser console inspection example. They do not define executable scripts, shell commands, or command construction from user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain no runnable Ruby, shell, Node, or Python entry points. The flagged backticks are documentation formatting and a non-persistent browser style check.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Detections
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss CSS layout behavior and browser rendering checks. They do not inspect the host, enumerate files, collect environment data, or fingerprint the system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is frontend layout guidance. No command, API, or instruction requests host reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Third-Party URL
low_findings.description: The skill includes an optional suggestion to mention a hosted AI workflow tool at an external URL. This is not automatic network access, but it can route users to a third-party service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URL is directly present in the skill text. Risk is limited because there is no code that sends requests or transfers user data.

content.user_title: Convert Pencil Designs Into Frontend Code
content.value_statement: Design handoffs often lose spacing, typography, and responsive intent during implementation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to inspect Pencil files and produce faithful frontend code.
actual_capabilities: Guides inspection of Pencil artboards, handoff nodes, and named node IDs.
Maps layout, spacing, typography, shadows, radii, and image fills into frontend code.
Preserves responsive behavior from provided Pencil artboards and handoff notes.
Encourages semantic components, CSS variables, and reusable frontend structure.
Defines visual verification steps using screenshots and computed browser styles.
limitations: Requires access to the Pencil files, node IDs, screenshots, or exported assets.
Does not include an executable converter or standalone parser for .pen files.
Final fidelity depends on available fonts, assets, and project framework constraints.
May need manual judgment when browser rendering differs from Pencil rendering.

use_cases.title: Implement a Product Mockup
use_cases.description: Turn a Pencil artboard into responsive application code while keeping layout, assets, and typography aligned.

use_cases.title: Repair Visual Drift
use_cases.description: Compare browser output against Pencil screenshots and correct spacing, font, image, or breakpoint differences.

use_cases.title: Create Reusable Design Tokens
use_cases.description: Extract repeated colors, radii, shadows, widths, and typography values into maintainable theme tokens.

prompt_templates.title: Implement One Artboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pencil-to-code skill to implement this Pencil artboard as frontend code. Preserve spacing, typography, assets, and responsive behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Match Responsive Variants
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Pencil handoff nodes and all provided artboard sizes. Build the component so each breakpoint matches the design screenshots.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Visual Fidelity
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my current page against the Pencil design. Identify layout, font, image, and spacing mismatches, then update the code.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Into Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the implemented Pencil design and extract repeated visual values into components and theme tokens without changing the rendered result.

output_examples.input: A Pencil mobile artboard with a fixed foreground column, image background, and button stack.
output_examples.output: A responsive frontend screen that keeps the column width fixed, preserves the background crop, and matches text styles.

output_examples.input: A visual fidelity bug where desktop cards stretch wider than the Pencil handoff.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis of the incorrect width behavior and an implementation update that restores the intended fixed card layout.

output_examples.input: A Pencil design with repeated rows, shared colors, and multiple typography styles.
output_examples.output: Reusable UI components with documented spacing, color, radius, and typography tokens based on the design.

best_practices: Provide all target artboards, node IDs, screenshots, and exported assets before implementation starts.
Verify output at each supplied artboard size, not only common responsive breakpoints.
Use computed browser styles when typography or spacing does not visually match Pencil.
anti_patterns: Do not approximate logos, photos, or decorative assets when real exports are available.
Do not stretch fixed foreground columns just because the viewport has extra width.
Do not rely on screenshots alone when handoff nodes define exact responsive behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically convert .pen files?
faq.answer: No. It guides an AI coding agent through careful inspection, implementation, and visual verification.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Use Pencil files, named node IDs, screenshots, handoff notes, exported assets, and target viewport sizes.

faq.question: Can it handle responsive designs?
faq.answer: Yes. It tells the agent to follow provided artboards and handoff rules for each responsive state.

faq.question: Does it replace designer review?
faq.answer: No. Human review is still useful for subjective rendering differences and intentional deviations.

faq.question: Is there a security concern?
faq.answer: The audit found no malicious behavior. A third-party promotional URL is present and should be reviewed before publishing.
